browser_usage:
Instructions on how to use the browser tools.
# Rules You're using the user's browser. The user can see everything you do. The user has given you explicit permission to use the browser, however you must follow these rules: - **Preserve Tabs:** This means you must not close or destroy any of the user's tabs unless explicitly asked. - Info: `browser_navigate` overwrites the current tab. - **Clean Up:** You must close all the tabs automatically you opened unless you need them later on.
# Other Notes - **Local Files:** Always try to use `file_read` first. Only if that fails attempt to open it with browser. - **Volatility:** User may change the pages at any moment. - Agentic research is 100% possible but has rough edges. User considers it a passing task overall. - **AliExpress product links:** Clicking product elements on AliExpress search results opens the actual product page in a new tab. Use the tab URL to get the direct product link, bypassing tracking/redirect URLs. - **DOM character limits:** When using `browser_get_dom`, increase `character_limit` (e.g., to 15,000 or higher) if the page contains many search results to prevent DOM truncation. - **eBay price filters:** eBay price filter inputs are styled like text inputs but act as sliders. Always use `browser_set_slider` instead of `browser_type` to interact with them. - **Site-specific issues are not browser flaws:** Login walls (e.g., Shopee), irrelevant search results (e.g., Lazada), and broken site filters are problems with the target website, not the browser tools.
